subject: Geography
title: The Appalachians
description: A fast breakdown of The Appalachians for builders.

hook.text: The Appalachians are among Earth's oldest mountains, formed 480 million years ago.

buildup.text: They once rivaled the Himalayas in height, towering above an ancient supercontinent.

discovery.text: Half a billion years of erosion ground them to gentle, forested ridges.

twist.text: Rich coal deposits in the Appalachians fueled America's Industrial Revolution.

climax.text: The same coal that built fortunes left behind scarred landscapes and polluted water.

punchline.text: Ancient giants worn down to gentle hills with heavy scars.

quiz.question: How old are the Appalachian Mountains?
